channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: ATO Warns Taxpayers to Avoid Misinformation and Declare All Income
item.description: The Australian Taxation Office (ATO) is stepping up scrutiny this tax season, focusing on work-related deductions, expenses, and undeclared income. With an increase in online 'tax hacks' and AI-genera...

item.title: Nairobi Woman Falls Victim to Age-Old Phone Theft Trick
item.description: A young lady in Nairobi has become the latest victim of a classic conman’s trick, losing her phone after encountering a man posing as someone who found money. The incident, which quickly went viral on...

item.title: Health Canada approves first generic semaglutide for Type 2 diabetes
item.description: Health Canada has approved the first generic version of the brand-name drug Ozempic, making Canada the first G7 country to authorize a generic form of semaglutide. The injectable medication, manufactu...

item.title: Philippine Coast Guard Rescues Seven Human Trafficking Victims in Tawi-Tawi
item.description: Seven individuals suspected to be victims of human trafficking were rescued by the Philippine Coast Guard in Bongao, Tawi-Tawi on Monday. Authorities intercepted the victims aboard a commercial vessel...

item.title: Rapid Growth of Solar Energy Offers Hope Amid Rising Energy Costs
item.description: As global energy costs soar, the rapid expansion and declining cost of renewable energy offer a bright spot. Between 2022 and 2024, the amount of solar energy installed worldwide doubled, and in the f...

item.title: UAE Cybercrime Laws Make Sharing Unverified Online Content Risky
item.description: In the United Arab Emirates, sharing online content such as screenshots, videos, or social media posts can carry serious legal consequences. Existing cybercrime laws, particularly Article 52, criminal...

item.title: Licensed Protection Agent Surrenders After Road Accident Incident
item.description: A licensed protection agent, Joey Sabando, has surrendered to the Makati Police following a road accident involving a pickup truck along Chino Roces Avenue in Barangay Fort Bonifacio, Taguig. Sabando,...

item.title: Third Accomplice Pleads Guilty in 2002 Jam Master Jay Murder Case
item.description: Jay Bryant, 52, admitted in federal court in Brooklyn that he assisted in the 2002 murder of Jason Mizell, known as Jam Master Jay of Run-DMC. Bryant confessed to helping two men enter Mizell's record...

item.title: Man approaches Odisha bank with sister's remains; bank requests paperwork
item.description: In an unusual and distressing incident in Odisha, a man reportedly brought the skeleton of his deceased sister to a local bank, reportedly seeking some official paperwork or documentation. This bizarr...

item.title: Poll Shows UCP Maintains Strong Approval in Alberta, NDP Support Declines
item.description: Three years into her mandate, Alberta Premier Danielle Smith and her United Conservative Party (UCP) continue to enjoy strong public support, according to the latest Janet Brown Opinion Research poll ...

item.title: Government Confirms No Disruption at Freedom Day Celebrations
item.description: On 27 April 2026, during South Africa's National Freedom Day celebrations in the Free State, reports circulated suggesting a disruption occurred when an individual briefly approached President Cyril R...

item.title: King Charles III Addresses Congress, Highlighting US-UK Relations and Global Challenges
item.description: King Charles III delivered a historic address to the US Congress, the first by a British monarch since Queen Elizabeth II in 1991, with the aim of strengthening US-UK relations and addressing current ...

item.title: Supreme Court Receives Petition Seeking Removal of IPS Ajay Pal Sharma as Bengal Poll Observer
item.description: A petition has been filed in the Supreme Court requesting the urgent removal of IPS Ajay Pal Sharma from his role as police observer in South 24 Parganas, Bengal, ahead of the state elections. The pet...

item.title: King Charles emphasises NATO, Ukraine and climate in address to US Congress
item.description: King Charles III delivered a significant speech to the US Congress, marking the first royal address in 35 years, during a state visit coinciding with the 250th anniversary of American independence. Th...

item.title: Xiaomi Launches REDMI A7 Pro with Big Display, Strong Battery, and Latest Features
item.description: Xiaomi don officially launch the REDMI A7 Pro for Naija market, wey dey bring beta performance for the REDMI A series this 2026. The phone get one big 6.9-inch display wey go make scrolling, video wat...

item.title: Austrian man pleads guilty to plotting IS-inspired terrorist attack on Taylor Swift concert in Vienna
item.description: An Austrian man, identified as a 21-year-old named Beran A. or Beran Aliji, has pleaded guilty in court to plotting a terrorist attack targeting Taylor Swift's Eras Tour concert at the Ernst-Happel-St...

item.title: Elizabeth Taylor and Richard Burton Star in Surreal 1968 Adaptation of Tennessee Williams' Boom!
item.description: Boom! is a 1968 cinematic adaptation of Tennessee Williams' play The Milk Train Doesn’t Stop Here Anymore, directed by Joseph Losey and starring Elizabeth Taylor and Richard Burton. The film follows F...

item.title: Waste management challenges and reform opportunities in Ajegunle, Lagos
item.description: One recent study wey dem do for Ajegunle, Ikorodu, Lagos State, don shine light on heavy wahala wey dey for waste management for the area. Di research, wey African Cities Research Consortium, Rethinki...

item.title: Harry Roque denies tax evasion complaints filed by BIR
item.description: Former presidential spokesman Harry Roque has publicly dismissed as baseless the tax evasion complaints lodged against him, his wife, and their legal counsel, Percival Ortega, before the Department of...

item.title: EFCC Chairman Urges Nigerian Universities to Adopt AI for Better Transparency and Fraud Prevention
item.description: The Executive Chairman of the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC), Ola Olukoyede, don talk say Artificial Intelligence (AI) dey very important for improving transparency, accountability an...
